revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[Miwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]

The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[Miwa Shrine]]

{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."

It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."

{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."

{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==

The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."

== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[Miwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[Miwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]

{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."

== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[Miwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[Miwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]

{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=draft}}, [[Chichibu]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|12=draft}}, [[Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|12=draft}}, [[Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社|12=draft}}, [[Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)|12=draft}}, [[Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|12=draft}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)|12=draft}}, [[Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)|12=draft}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)|12=draft}}, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)|12=draft}}, [[Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社|12=draft}}, [[Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|ja|今宮戎神社|12=draft}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikutama Shrine|ja|坐摩神社|12=draft}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[Hibara Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=draft}}, [[Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社|12=draft}}, [[Izumo, Shimane]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社|12=draft}}, [[Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮|12=draft}}, [[Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社|12=draft}}, [[Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社|12=draft}}, [[Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺|12=draft}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社|12=draft}}, [[Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[Hibara Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>

revision.text:
{{混同|三柱鳥居}} [[画像:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[三峯神社]]の三ツ鳥居]] '''三ツ鳥居'''（みつとりい）は、[[鳥居]]の様式の1つである。'''三輪鳥居'''（みわとりい）ともいう。
1つの[[明神鳥居]]の両脇に、小規模な2つの鳥居を組み合わせたものである<ref>[[近藤豊 (建築学者)|近藤豊]]著『古建築の細部意匠』大河出版 1972年</ref>。<!--柱が4本になるため、'''四脚鳥居'''と呼ばれることもある。（四脚鳥居と呼ばれるものは、両部鳥居のみのようです。もう一度、出典元をご確認いただければと思います。）-->
==日本の三ツ鳥居== <!--地方公共団体コード順--> * [[三峯神社]]（[[埼玉県]][[秩父市]]） * [[三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|三輪神社]]（[[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]） - ギンザコマツ西館屋上 * [[牛嶋神社]]（東京都[[墨田区]]） * [[和納三社神社]]（[[新潟県]][[新潟市]][[西蒲区]]） * [[神明宮 (長岡市)|神明宮]]（新潟県[[長岡市]]） * [[美和神社 (長野市)|美和神社]]（[[長野県]][[長野市]]） * [[三輪神社 (揖斐川町)|三輪神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[揖斐郡]][[揖斐川町]]） * [[愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)|愛宕神社]]（[[静岡県]][[静岡市]]） * [[神神社 (藤枝市)|神神社]]（静岡県[[藤枝市]]） * [[三輪神社 (名古屋市)|三輪神社]]（[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[中区 (名古屋市)|中区]]） * [[伊香具神社]]（[[滋賀県]][[長浜市]]） * [[今宮戎神社]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[浪速区]]） * [[坐摩神社]]（大阪府大阪市[[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]） * [[大神神社]]（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]） - 三ツ鳥居の形式ができた年代や由来については不明である<ref>大神神社の公式ホームページの「境内の案内－三ツ鳥居」より。三ツ鳥居の模型の写真も掲載されている。</ref>。なお、[[三輪山]]山中の[[禁足地]]にある三ツ鳥居は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 * [[大神神社#本社近辺|檜原神社]]（奈良県桜井市） - 大神神社の本社周辺にある摂社 * [[長浜神社]]（[[島根県]][[出雲市]]） * [[赤嵜神社]]（[[山口県]][[山陽小野田市]]） * [[白峰宮]]（[[香川県]][[坂出市]]） - 赤い大鳥居井の両脇の小鳥居は、包み込むように少し内側に閉じている。 * [[加麻良神社]]（香川県[[観音寺市]]） * [[八幡古表神社]]（[[福岡県]][[築上郡]][[吉富町]]） - 境内には「明治九年奉献」と「平成四年奉献」の二つの三ツ鳥居が見られる。 * [[宝満神宮寺]]（福岡県[[糟屋郡]][[須恵町]][[大字上須恵]]） - 境内にある「和の宮」の磐座を赤い三ツ鳥居が守っている。 * [[唐泉山#八天神社・山岳信仰|八天神社]]（[[佐賀県]][[嬉野市]]） - 三ツ鳥居は「[[殷墟|殷]]の鳥居（瀛の鳥居）」と呼ばれる。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[大神神社#本社近辺|檜原神社]]（奈良県） 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[三輪神社 (名古屋市)|三輪神社]]（名古屋） Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[三峯神社]]（埼玉県） 7900b1.jpg|[[白峰宮]] （香川県） Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[加麻良神社]]（香川県） Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|三ツ鳥居の正式な通り方<ref>三輪神社（名古屋市）の公式ホームページより</ref> </gallery>
==脚注== {{reflist}}
==関連項目== *[[三柱鳥居]] - 「三ツ鳥居と三柱鳥居は、その意義も形式も全く異なる」と、[[大神教会]]の三柱鳥居の前の立て札に書かれている。 <!--*[[両部鳥居]]も四脚鳥居と呼ばれることがある。三ツ鳥居も両部鳥居も鳥居の柱が4本ある。--> *[[三輪神社]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ 大神神社]（公式サイト） *[http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ 三峯神社]（公式サイト） *[http://miwajinnjya.com/ 名古屋 大須 三輪神社]（公式サイト）
{{日本の建築}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みつとりい}} [[Category:鳥居]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Chichibu|Chichibu]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Shrine|qq=|1=Ushijima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97280705}}, [[:en:Sumida, Tokyo|Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wano Sansha Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Niigata|Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nagaoka, Niigata|Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608851}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357405}}, [[:en:Ibigawa, Gifu|Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinkami Shrine|qq=|1=Miwa Shrine (Fujieda)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134926697}}, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60297838}}, [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi|Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagu Shrine|qq=|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}, [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ([[:en:Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]], [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikutama Shrine]] ([[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikutama Shrine]], [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Shrine|qq=|1=Nagahama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653315}}, [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi|Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Jingu|lt=Shiramine Jingu|lt=Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakaide, Kagawa|Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanonji, Kagawa|Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangobyo Shrine|qq=|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}}, [[:en:Yoshitomi, Fukuoka|Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōman Jingū-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ureshino, Saga|Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]
{{Japanese architecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

<!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mitsu torii}} {{wikidata link|Q11354046}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==

Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Shrine|1=Ushijima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97280705}}, <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Shrine|1=Ushijima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97280705}}, <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Chichibu|Chichibu]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Shrine|1=Ushijima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97280705}}, Niigata|Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, based on wikidata|WD=Q11357405}}, [[:en:Ibigawa, Gifu|Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

<!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mitsu torii}} {{wikidata link|Q11354046}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Torii]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Chichibu|Chichibu]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō, Tokyo]]) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Shrine|1=Ushijima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97280705}}, [[:en:Sumida, Tokyo|Sumida, Tokyo]]) * [[Wano Sansha Shrine]] ({{ill|Wano Sansha Shrine|ja|和納三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wano Sansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Niigata|Nishi-ku, Niigata]]) * [[Shimmei Shrine (Nagaoka)|Shimmei Shrine]] ({{ill|Shimmei Shrine|ja|神明宮 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nagaoka, Niigata|Nagaoka, Niigata]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608851}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357405}}, [[:en:Ibigawa, Gifu|Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinkami Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine (Fujieda)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134926697}}, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60297838}}, [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi|Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagu Shrine|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}, [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]]) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ([[:en:Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]], [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikutama Shrine]] ([[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikutama Shrine]], [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]]) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]]) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Shrine|1=Nagahama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653315}}, [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]]) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi|Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Jingu|lt=Shiramine Jingu|lt=Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakaide, Kagawa|Sakaide, Kagawa]]) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanonji, Kagawa|Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangobyo Shrine|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}}, [[:en:Yoshitomi, Fukuoka|Yoshitomi, Fukuoka]]) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōman Jingū-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ureshino, Saga|Ureshino, Saga]]) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|[[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.oomiwa.or.jp/ Official Ōmiwa Shrine website] * [http://www.mitsuminejinja.or.jp/ Official Mitsumine Shrine website] * [http://miwajinnjya.com/ Official Miwa Shrine website (Nagoya)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354046]] Wikidata name: mitsutorii [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]]
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==

Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]]
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==

<!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--><!--Mitsutorii--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mitsu torii}} {{wikidata link|Q11354046}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Torii]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]]
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mitsu torii]][[ja:三ツ鳥居]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三ツ鳥居]]
{{Distinguish|Three-Pillar Torii}} [[File:Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mitsumine Shrine]]'s Mitsutorii]] '''Mitsutorii''' (三ツ鳥居), also called '''Miwa Torii''' (三輪鳥居), is a style of [[torii]], or Shinto gate.
It consists of a central [[Myōjin torii]] flanked by two smaller torii on either side<ref>[[Yutaka Kondo (architectural scholar)|Yutaka Kondo]] (1972). ''Details of Old Architectural Design''. Taiga Publishing.</ref>.
== Mitsutorii in Japan ==
* [[Mitsumine Shrine]] ({{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Chichibu|Chichibu]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}) * [[Miwa Shrine (Chūō, Tokyo)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (東京都中央区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|en|Chūō, Tokyo|WD=Q212704|lt=Chūō, Tokyo|1=Chūō, Tokyo}}) – Located on the rooftop of Ginza Komatsu West Building. * [[Ushijima Shrine]] ({{ill|Ushijima Shrine|ja|牛嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Shrine|1=Ushijima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97280705}}, {{ill|Sumida, Tokyo|en|Sumida, (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagaoka, Niigata|en|Nagaoka, Niigata|WD=Q540312|lt=Nagaoka, Niigata|1=Nagaoka, Niigata}}) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608851}}, [[Nagano City, Nagano]]) * [[Miwa Shrine (Ibigawa)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (揖斐川町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357405}}, [[:en:Ibigawa, Gifu|Ibigawa, Gifu]]) * [[Atago Shrine (Shizuoka)|Atago Shrine]] ({{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社 (静岡県静岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Shizuoka City, Shizuoka]]) * [[Jinkami Shrine]] ({{ill|Jinkami Shrine|ja|神神社 (藤枝市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinkami Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine (Shizuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134926697}}, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda, Shizuoka|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}) * [[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] ({{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|三輪神社 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60297838}}, [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi|Nagoya, Aichi]]) * [[Ikagu Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagu Shrine|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}, {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama, Shiga|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}) * [[Imamiya Ebisu Shrine]] ({{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|en|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|WD=Q11376859|lt=Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|1=Imamiya Ebisu Shrine}}, [[Naniwa, Osaka]]) * [[Ikukunitama Shrine|Ikutama Shrine]] ({{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikutama Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}}, [[Chūō, Osaka]]) * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}) – The origin and development of the Mitsutorii style are unclear<ref>The official website of Ōmiwa Shrine includes a model of the Mitsutorii style.</ref>. The Mitsutorii in the sacred mountain {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa|1=Mount Miwa}} is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} ({{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}) – A sub-shrine near Ōmiwa Shrine. * [[Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|Nagahama Shrine]] ({{ill|Nagahama Shrine|ja|長浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Shrine|1=Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653315}}, {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}}) * [[Akasaki Shrine]] ({{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤嵜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi|Sanyo-Onoda, Yamaguchi]]) * [[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] ({{ill|Shiramine Jingu|lt=Shiramine Jingu|lt=Shiramine Shrine|ja|白峰宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakaide, Kagawa|en|Sakaide, Kagawa|WD=Q855059|lt=Sakaide, Kagawa|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}) – The smaller flanking torii slightly curve inward. * [[Kamara Shrine]] ({{ill|Kamara Shrine|ja|加麻良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanonji, Kagawa|Kanonji, Kagawa]]) * [[Hachimangobyo Shrine]] ({{ill|Hachimangobyo Shrine|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangobyo Shrine|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}}, {{ill|Yoshitomi, Fukuoka|en|Yoshitomi, Fukuoka|WD=Q1205793|lt=Yoshitomi, Fukuoka|1=Yoshitomi, Fukuoka}}) – Two Mitsutorii gates were donated in the Meiji and Heisei periods. * [[Hōman Jingū-ji]] ({{ill|Hōman Jingū-ji|ja|宝満神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōman Jingū-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Suemachi, Kasuya District, Fukuoka]]) – Protects a sacred rock formation called "Wa no Miya" with a red Mitsutorii. * [[Yahata Shrine (Ureshino)|Yahata Shrine]] ({{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ureshino, Saga|en|Ureshino, Saga|WD=Q634130|lt=Ureshino, Saga|1=Ureshino, Saga}}) – The Mitsutorii is called the "Yin Torii."
== Gallery == <gallery> Hibara-jinja, torii-2.jpg|{{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Nara) 名古屋三輪神社.jpg|[[Miwa Shrine (Nagoya)|Miwa Shrine]] (Nagoya) Mitsumine Shrine Triple Torii.jpg|[[Mitsumine Shrine]] (Saitama) 7900b1.jpg|[[Shiramine Shrine (Sakaide)|Shiramine Shrine]] (Kagawa) Kamarazinzya01.jpg|[[Kamara Shrine]] (Kagawa) Mitutorii0ɨ.jpg|Proper way to pass through a Mitsutorii<ref>The official website of Miwa Shrine in Nagoya.</ref> </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[Three-Pillar Torii]] – Unlike Mitsutorii, the significance and structure of this design differ, as explained on a sign in front of the [[Ōmiwa Kyōkai]]'s Three-Pillar Torii. * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}
